message:
fix(btree): handle oversized interior cell replacement via structural rebalance

Previously replace_interior_cell refused to handle cases where the
replacement key was too large for the existing interior page, returning
an error ("interior replacement requires rebalance; refusing partial
update"). This caused B-tree deletes to fail when the successor key
exceeded the interior node's page capacity.

Fix by adding a rebalance path in the "doesn't fit" branch:
1. Remove the old cell from the pointer array
2. Rewrite the page header with updated cell count/content offset
3. Free the old overflow chain
4. Call balance_for_insert to insert the new cell via the standard
   split/merge path, triggering a proper structural rebalance

Return type changed from Result<()> to Result<bool> — true indicates
a structural rebalance occurred. The caller in BtreeCursorOps::delete
handles this by re-seeking via index_seek to find the duplicate
successor in the leaf after the cursor stack is invalidated.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
